message:
feat(pty): migrate from smol to asupersync runtime with regression guards

Complete the async runtime migration for the PTY crate, replacing smol
with the project's own asupersync runtime abstraction layer.

Dev-dependency swap:
- Remove futures and smol workspace dev-dependencies
- Add asupersync workspace dev-dependency

Example migration (whoami_async.rs):
- Replace smol::block_on with RuntimeBuilder::current_thread().build()
- Replace smol::unblock with asupersync::runtime::spawn_blocking
- Replace smol::io::BufReader / smol::Unblock with std::io::BufReader
  inside spawn_blocking closures
- Update comments to reference asupersync

Regression guard tests (lib.rs, +90 lines):
- pty_manifest_excludes_smol_and_async_io_dependencies: reads Cargo.toml
  at test time and asserts smol/async-io are not present (ignoring
  comments), preventing accidental re-introduction
- pty_sources_and_examples_exclude_runtime_specific_async_tokens: scans
  all .rs files under src/ and examples/ for direct smol:: or async_io::
  usage, catching any runtime-specific code that bypasses the abstraction
- Helper functions: contains_non_comment_token, collect_rust_files
- Token strings split across format!() to avoid self-detection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -39,5 +39,4 @@ winapi = { workspace=true, features = [
 winreg.workspace = true
 
 [dev-dependencies]
-futures.workspace = true
-smol.workspace = true
+asupersync.workspace = true
